sourceMovie.title: The Ladykillers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Ladykillers for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ethan Coen keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 22 Jump Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ladykillers clicked for you, 22 Jump Street (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Swordfish
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ladykillers clicked for you, Swordfish (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Ladykillers?
faq.answer: Try 22 Jump Street, Swordfish, The Naked Gun—each shares DNA with The Ladykillers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Ladykillers?
faq.answer: The Ladykillers (2004) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Ladykillers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
